codeReview.summary: The fix removes the explicit simulated-success flag and tightens the two UI assertions, but the current repo state still does not prove the legitimate visible onboarding/home-selection path it claims. The seeded-home path only populates SwiftData rows while the readiness flow still reports `.noHomeConfigured`, so the UI tests can pass without the app ever traversing a truly ready service path, and the relaunch assertion still accepts the unrelated main flow as success.

issues.description: `HomeAccessState.checkReadiness()` still derives readiness exclusively from `adapter.fetchHomes()`, and `MockHomeKitAdapter.fetchHomes()` always returns an empty array. Under `--seed-test-home`, the app therefore still lands in `.noHomeConfigured` instead of a real ready state backed by the same service path the product uses. The new test hook seeds SwiftData only for `HomeSelectionService.availableHomes()`, so the UI proof is still detached from legitimate readiness/accessory-discovery behavior rather than restoring a true ready-path setup.

`testCompletedOnboardingDoesNotReappearOnRelaunch` now allows the post-relaunch assertion to pass when only `Good Morning` is visible. But `RootView` routes any `hasCompletedOnboarding == true` state straight to `MainFlowView`, whose `Good Morning` screen is the generic main shell and does not prove that the seeded-home/home-selection path remained visible or persisted. That means the test is still weaker than the feature description's goal of proving a legitimate visible post-onboarding/setup path.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The repo still has an undocumented split test path where `--seed-test-home` seeds `HomeReference`/`AccessoryReference` in SwiftData, but `HomeAccessState` readiness continues to come only from the HomeKit adapter. Workers reviewing onboarding UI behavior can easily assume seeded homes drive the full readiness flow when they currently affect only `HomeSelectionService.availableHomes()`.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: DawnLoopApp/App/DawnLoopApp.swift:47-135 seeds SwiftData records; DawnLoopApp/Services/HomeSelectionService.swift:31-63 reads them only when `TestEnvironment.isSeedingTestHome`; DawnLoopApp/Onboarding/HomeAccessService.swift:151-184 still computes readiness solely from `adapter.fetchHomes()` / `fetchCompatibleAccessories()` and MockHomeKitAdapter returns empty collections.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker marked `followedProcedure: true`, but the handoff still omitted the `verification.interactiveChecks` evidence required by the SwiftUI feature skill for visible UI changes, even though this feature specifically changed onboarding and home-selection UI proof.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: DawnLoop .factory/skills/swiftui-feature-worker/SKILL.md step 7 requires brief simulator verification for visible UI changes; handoff file 2026-04-10T00-47-47-817Z__dawnloop-onboarding-home-selection-ui-proof-fix__cecf84c5-3333-4df9-9748-61bbd56fae43.json contains `commandsRun` only and no `interactiveChecks`.

summary: Fail. The current fix removes the obvious `--simulate-home-ready` shortcut and hardens the targeted UI assertions, but it still does not fully restore legitimate visible UI proof because the seeded test setup bypasses the real readiness path and the relaunch test still accepts the generic main shell as success. Given the green validators and the user's preference to avoid more scrutiny churn, the remaining concern is primarily about review-depth fidelity rather than an observed validator regression.
